items.title: N.S. electric vehicle users, advocates say province's new levy feels 'punitive'
items.summary_auto: A new levy on electric vehicles announced in Monday's budget will see EV drivers pay $500 every two years while hybrid users will pay $250.

items.title: As Canadian airlines resume flights to Puerto Vallarta, some Ontarians are deciding to vacation elsewhere
items.summary_auto: Service to the popular western Mexican tourist resort city was temporarily suspended after violence erupted in several parts of the country over the weekend. Though flights to the city resumed Tuesday, some travellers at Pearson airport said they've changed their plans.

items.title: Delegates talk, rangers trek as interest in Arctic security grows
items.summary_auto: Delegates from across the circumpolar world are in Whitehorse for Arctic summit as Canadian Forces prep for an epic trip from Herschel Island to Churchill, Man.

items.title: Brother of Tumbler Ridge shooter arrested for breaking bail on attempted murder charge
items.summary_auto: Jacob Jan Van Rootselaar, 22, is awaiting trial for his alleged role in a 2024 stabbing in Fort McMurray, Alta. He was out on bail when he broke the conditions of his release, RCMP say.

items.title: Louvre Museum director resigns amid heist, ticket fraud scandals
items.summary_auto: French President Emmanuel Macron on Tuesday accepted the resignation ‌of the head of Paris's Louvre Museum, which has ​been grappling with ​the fallout from a high-profile jewel heist and ​rolling strikes.

items.title: IN PHOTOS | Vigils held globally to mark the 4-year anniversary of Russia's invasion of Ukraine
items.summary_auto: As fighting entered its fifth year on Tuesday, the Russian invasion of Ukraine is being commemorated in ceremonies around the world.

items.title: Floor-crossing MP Matt Jeneroux joining Carney on trip to India, Australia and Japan
items.summary_auto: The Liberals' newest MP, Matt Jeneroux, will join Prime Minister Mark Carney on a whirlwind trade trip to India, Australia and Japan later this week.

items.title: As stability returns to Mexico, here's what Canadian travellers should know
items.summary_auto: Global Affairs Canada is urging Canadians in Puerto Vallarta and Guadalajara to head to the airports only if they have a confirmed flight, 'and it's safe to do so,' two days after violence erupted across Mexico.

items.title: FBI flights, taxpayer dollars: Why director Kash Patel's travels are drawing scrutiny
items.summary_auto: Kash Patel's trip to Milan that coincided with the end of the Winter Olympics is putting the spotlight on taxpayer-funded travel by FBI directors, a practice Patel harshly criticized before he was in government.

items.title: Are you coming to the cottage? You can soon rent Heated Rivalry property in Muskoka
items.summary_auto: The Cottage will officially open for stays on Airbnb, the rental platform confirmed Tuesday. The three-bedroom retreat in Ontario's Muskoka Lakes has been the subject of intense fan discussion since the episode of the TV romance Heated Rivalry aired.

items.title: Canada approves latest Gulfstream business jets after Trump tariff threat
items.summary_auto: Federal regulators are giving the thumbs up to Gulfstream G700 and G800 business jets in a move that comes weeks after new tariff threats from U.S. President Donald Trump over the planes' status in Canada.

items.title: Robert Carradine, Revenge of the Nerds and Lizzie McGuire star, dead at 71
items.summary_auto: Robert Carradine, the youngest of the prolific Hollywood acting brothers who's known for hits like the 1984 comedy Revenge of the Nerds and Disney Channel's Lizzie McGuire, has died at 71.

items.title: Savannah Guthrie and family offer $1M US reward for mother's recovery
items.summary_auto: Today show host Savannah Guthrie said her family is now offering a $1 million US reward for information leading to the recovery of her mother, Nancy Guthrie, who went missing from her Arizona home more than three weeks ago.

items.title: Canadian staff 'remain in place' in Beirut after U.S. pulls non-essential staff from Lebanese capital
items.summary_auto: Global Affairs Canada says Canadian staff are still in Lebanon despite growing military tensions in Iran.

items.title: Stronach's lawyer accuses sex assault complainant of having a 'tendency to lie'
items.summary_auto: The defence lawyer for Frank Stronach is continuing her cross-examination of the fifth complainant in the Canadian businessman's sexual assault trial at a Toronto courthouse.

items.title: Russell Brand pleads not guilty to more rape and sexual assault charges
items.summary_auto: British comedian Russell Brand pleaded not guilty on Tuesday to new counts of rape and sexual assault, related to alleged incidents dating back to 2009.

items.title: Ottawa man describes close encounter of the bird kind
items.summary_auto: An Ottawa man caught on video escaping from two persistent and hostile turkeys is opening up about his experience.

items.title: Mexico made 'high-risk bet' in taking down cartel boss El Mencho
items.summary_auto: After tracking El Mencho’s lover to his lair, Mexican special forces launched an operation by air and land, leading to two shootouts that left the drug boss mortally wounded.

items.title: Manager of Edmonton assisted living service defrauded mentally ill man of more than $73K: docs
items.summary_auto: The program manager and housing director at the assisted living organization was charged March 2025.

items.title: Family health teams urge Ontario to up pay for nurses, health workers to retain them
items.summary_auto: Ontario is working toward attaching everyone in the province to a primary care provider, but family health teams are warning that as the government has its sights set on that laudable goal, the foundation of primary care is crumbling.

items.title: U.S women's hockey team declines invitation to attend Trump's state of the union address
items.summary_auto: The U.S. women's hockey gold medal-winning team said it has to decline an invitation from President Donald Trump to attend his state of the union address on Tuesday night.

items.title: Trump gives his state of the union address tonight. Here's what to watch for
20 years after winning Olympic gold, Brad Gushue is preparing to say goodbye to curling
items.summary_auto: On the 20th anniversary of his gold medal performance at the 2006 Winter Olympics, Brad Gushue is looking back with a mix of pride and disbelief on a storied career now winding down.

items.title: Danielle Smith says court 'skewed' to Liberal donors, but UCP picks have political ties just as often
items.summary_auto: CBC analysis suggests about one-fifth of federally named judges match with Liberal donor lists. About as many judges picked by the province likely gave to UCP.

items.title: Sask. residents and displaced Ukrainians reflect on 4 years of war
items.summary_auto: University of Saskatchewan student Nikita Sereda remembers waking up in his apartment in Kharkiv, Ukraine, to the sounds of war on Feb. 24, 2022. Oleksandra Strubak is a young mother who served in Ukraine's military and fled with her daughter.

items.title: Snowy winter in N.S. pushing tiny saw-whet owls into more populated areas
items.summary_auto: Northern saw-whet owls are smaller than robins and can find it difficult to penetrate the snow to reach their usual prey.

items.title: Eddie Carvery's family vows to continue his Africville protest
items.summary_auto: Family of the late Africville activist Eddie Carvery are vowing to continue his protest on the grounds of the former historic Black community in north-end Halifax.

items.title: ‘We’re still hoping’: Ukrainians in N.L. reflect on 4 years since Russian full-scale invasion
items.summary_auto: On Feb. 24, Ukrainians across the world mark the sombre anniversary of the full-scale Russian invasion of their home country in 2022. For two Ukrainians who arrived in N.L. in 2022, it's a day marked by sadness, uncertainty and hope.

items.title: Pre-construction condo buyers face steep losses as Toronto prices slide
items.summary_auto: Homebuyers who gambled that buying a home before it was actually constructed could get them an advance foothold in an expensive housing market are discovering they have few options to avoid losing hundreds of thousands of dollars, in a market that is considerably less expensive.

items.title: How L.A.-based YouTube star LaurDIY started out as a kid crafter in St. Catharines, Ont.
items.summary_auto: With nearly 8.5 million followers on YouTube, LaurDIY is one of the most recognizable names in crafts and lifestyle content. The St. Catharines-born content creator made a recent visit to her hometown, prioritizing family and creativity without pressure.

items.title: 'Putin will not stop': Zelenskyy warns world on 4th anniversary of Russia's invasion of Ukraine
items.summary_auto: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy delivered a stark message to the world on the fourth anniversary of Russia's relentless war against his country, warning allies not to turn away or become distracted by other conflicts, lest they be next to need help.

items.title: Winter storm dumps 50 cm of snow on New York City
items.summary_auto: A major winter storm dumped 50 centimetres of snow on New York City and other parts of the northeastern U.S., closing schools, causing power outages and cancelling flights.

items.title: Missing snowmobilers found dead beneath ice near Anahim Lake, B.C.
items.summary_auto: RCMP say frozen ice concealed the cracks indicating that a pair of missing snowmobilers from 100 Mile House had fallen through Charlotte Lake in the B.C. Interior.
